chunks.heading: When does eligibility to participate in the UNJSPF commence, and is participation optional?
chunks.text: Eligibility to participate in the United Nations Joint Staff Pension Fund (UNJSPF) commences upon entering the service of a UNJSPF member organization with an appointment of six months or longer OR, if earlier, upon completing six months of continuous service without an interruption of more than 30 days, provided that participation in the Fund is not expressly excluded by the terms of the appointment. Contributions are automatically deducted from your salary each month and reported to the Fund. Your employing organization pays twice the amount of your contributions to the Fund.

chunks.heading: Is participation in the Fund optional?
chunks.text: No, participation in the Fund is mandatory once you fulfill the requirements of article 21(a) of the UNJSPF Regulations.

chunks.heading: How do I enroll?
chunks.text: No action is required on your part. If you are a staff member of a United Nations family organization, your employing organization informs the Fund of your contractual status and family composition by issuing a personnel action (PA) form. The Fund then enrolls you as a participant by assigning a pension number, which is subsequently relayed to you through your Administrative/Executive Office. If you are a staff member of another member organization, the Secretary of your organization's Staff Pension Committee (SPC) handles your participation in the Fund and will take the necessary action for your participation to be communicated to the Fund. Note: Your Unique Identification number (assigned by the Fund) is different from your index number (assigned by your employing organization).

chunks.heading: Do I have access to the information contained in my pension record?
chunks.text: The Fund prepares an Annual Pension Statement for each participant that contains the personal and financial data as reported by your employing organization for the prior year. You can access your Annual Pension Statement through your Member Self-Service account. It is important that you review the Annual Pension Statement and contact your employing organization and/or the Fund should any of the data generated appear to be inaccurate. The Annual Pension Statement reflects data reported for the previous year(s); for example, the annual statement issued in May 2022 contains data reported through 31 December 2021, and any change in your circumstances in 2023 is reflected in the next annual statement available in May 2024.

chunks.heading: What coverage do I get under the UNJSPF and how is it determined?
chunks.text: You are covered against death and disability from the beginning of your participation. Upon separation from service, you may become entitled to a pension benefit based on your age and length of contributory service. For retirement, death and disability benefits, additional factors such as final average remuneration and a rate of accumulation are also taken into account. The rate of accumulation is the rate at which your retirement benefit accumulates for each year of contributory service, set out in Article 28(b) of the UNJSPF Regulations and Rules. The maximum accumulation rate attainable is 70%.

chunks.heading: How are my contributions to the UNJSPF calculated?
chunks.text: Once you become a participant, contributions are automatically deducted from your salary and reported to the Fund on your behalf. The current deduction is 7.9% of your pensionable remuneration rate. Your employing organization's portion is twice your amount, i.e. 15.8% of the pensionable remuneration rate. Thus a total of 23.7% of your pensionable remuneration is contributed to the UNJSPF.

chunks.heading: Do I earn interest on my contributions?
chunks.text: Yes. Interest accrues at the rate of 3.25% per year while you remain an active participant. Interest accrues only until your separation date and does not accrue on current year contributions.

chunks.heading: Does the Fund extend personal loans to participants?
chunks.text: No. There are no provisions in the Fund's Regulations and Rules for making loans, or advance payments in anticipation of separation benefits to participants. Furthermore, an active participant cannot receive any benefits and/or make any withdrawals from their pension contributions.

chunks.heading: I understand that the UNJSPF is a defined benefit plan. What does that mean?
chunks.text: As a defined benefit plan, the UNJSPF provides guaranteed benefits based on specific formulae set out in the Fund's Regulations. Your pension depends on factors such as final average remuneration at the time of retirement, length of contributory service, age and rate of accumulation.

chunks.heading: There was a period prior to the commencement of my participation during which I did not contribute to the Fund; can I pay contributions for that prior period of non-contributory service upon becoming a participant?
chunks.text: Validation is an option which allows you to pay contributions retroactively for period(s) of employment, prior to the date on which your participation commenced, when you were not eligible to participate in the Fund. Also, service under Special Service Agreement (SSA) or employment as a consultant or individual contractor does not confer staff member status, and does not allow for UNJSPF participation. A former consultant who subsequently becomes a staff member and a participant in the Fund cannot validate previous service under an SSA agreement or under contracts for consultants or individual contractors.

chunks.heading: If I separate from service and a benefit is paid to me, may I return the payment that I received if I am subsequently reemployed with a member organization and re-enter the Fund?
chunks.text: If you participated in the Fund in the past and upon separation were paid a withdrawal settlement, and you again become a participant, you can restore your most recent prior period of contributory service under Article 24 of the Fund's Regulations, making it contributory. Restoration on the same terms is also available if prior to 1 April 2007 you elected or were deemed to have elected a deferred retirement benefit not yet in payment at the time of your election to restore. If you elected or were deemed to have elected a deferred retirement benefit on or after 1 April 2007, you may also elect to restore contributory service equivalent to the actuarial value of the deferred retirement benefit under Article 24 bis of the Fund's Regulations. You must apply for restoration within 12 months from your date of re-entry into Fund participation.

chunks.heading: Can a break in service be made pensionable?
chunks.text: A break in service cannot be made pensionable under any circumstances.

chunks.heading: How does my part-time status affect my participation/pension entitlement?
chunks.text: You remain a participant in the Fund if you are on at least 50% employment status. The amount of the benefit resulting from part-time employment would be reduced by the ratio which it bears to full employment, taking into account your final average remuneration, number of years of contributory service, age at the time of separation, etc.

chunks.heading: What happens if I take a period of special leave without pay (SLWOP)?
chunks.text: You have two options. To pay contributions to the Fund during the period of SLWOP: the period would be considered contributory service; you must arrange this with the payroll office of your employing organization (not the UNJSPF) BEFORE starting the SLWOP period, paying both your own and the organization's share of contributions concurrently with the leave — retroactive arrangements are not permitted. If contributions are paid concurrently, participation continues and you accrue future pension rights; however, if you later take a withdrawal settlement, you would NOT receive back the money you paid as the organization's share. Not to pay contributions during SLWOP: you continue to be considered a participant (continuous participation) for a maximum of 36 months but do NOT accrue pension rights for this non-contributory period; you are deemed to have separated from the UNJSPF after 36 months of SLWOP without concurrent contributions. Spouses married and/or children born during a non-contributing SLWOP period would not be covered for potential survivor's benefits until you again become a contributing member.

chunks.heading: May I remain on SLWOP indefinitely?
chunks.text: No. A participant is deemed to have separated from the Fund upon completing a consecutive period of 36 months of SLWOP without concurrent contributions, in accordance with Article 25(a) of the Fund's Regulations. To re-enter the Fund, you would have to satisfy the requirements for participation once again.

chunks.heading: What happens if I am seconded or loaned to another UNJSPF member organization?
chunks.text: The organizations concerned would forward the related documentation to the Fund and your pension record would be updated accordingly. Your participation would be continuous without any break in service.

chunks.heading: What happens if I separate from a UNJSPF member organization and join another organization or rejoin the same organization?
chunks.text: If you join/rejoin the same or another UNJSPF member organization within 36 months of your separation without a benefit being paid, your participation is deemed to be continuing; inform your employing organization of your prior participation and provide your Unique Identification Number (UID) — your records may show a break in service between contracts, but no additional action is required since the organizations report separation and re-entry dates. If you join/rejoin after 36 months of separation, a new pension account will be opened upon re-entering the Fund; contributions for the new period cannot be added to the previous account, and you will have two separate pension accounts, with a benefit from each calculated separately upon final separation.

chunks.heading: If my new/former employer is not a member organization of the Fund, am I allowed to transfer my pension rights into/from the Fund?
chunks.text: It depends on the organization from which or to which you are transferring. Transfer of pension rights into or from the UNJSPF is permitted only if a transfer agreement between the organization concerned and the UNJSPF is in effect. A listing of UNJSPF Transfer Agreements is available on the Fund's website.
